summary:
• Education Investment: Hamilton County is advancing inclusive education with innovative projects at three schools, including an ADA-accessible sensory garden at Hunter Middle School.
  • Recreation Impact: Due to rain, mountain bike trails at ESNP and Walden's Ridge Park are temporarily closed, affecting outdoor recreational activities.
  • Cultural Development: The city's cultural infrastructure is at a critical juncture, emphasizing the need for continued investment in arts and humanities to sustain community systems.

items.title: Three schools. Three innovative projects. One shared investment in Hamilton County’s students. 🌿 Hunter Middle School –...
items.content_text:
Three schools. Three innovative projects. One shared investment in Hamilton County’s students.
 🌿 Hunter Middle School – Cason’s Courtyard, featuring an inclusive sensory garden and ADA-accessible playground.
 🌳 Calvin Donaldson Elementary – An outdoor learning campus.
 🌲 Big Ridge Elementary – An Outdoor Learning Center pavilion and arboretum.
 Each school will receive a $40,000 Active Outdoor Spaces Grant to help bring these projects to life.

items.title: Our cultural infrastructure is at a crossroads. Decades of public investment in the arts, humanities, museums, libraries...
items.content_text:
Our cultural infrastructure is at a crossroads.
 Decades of public investment in the arts, humanities, museums, libraries, arts education, and federal research have built the systems communities rely on to preserve history, support artists, and educate the next generation.
 Proposed federal funding cuts would weaken that infrastructure right when it's needed most.
 There's also a chance to strengthen it: the bipartisan HBCU Arts Act would expand arts education, cultural preservation, and creative career pathways at HBCUs. 🎓🎨
 Before Congress leaves for August recess, we need to act.
 👉 Contact Congress now: https://bit.ly/4uXN8MF
 #AmericansfortheArts #ArtsAdvocacy #HBCUArtsAct

items.title: Career Opportunity in #EastRidgeTN!  🚧 🚜 Are you searching for a career where your work makes a difference?  The City...
items.content_text:
Career Opportunity in #EastRidgeTN! 
 🚧 🚜
 Are you searching for a career where your work makes a difference? 
 The City of East Ridge Street Department is seeking a dependable, hardworking Equipment Operator to help maintain the roads, infrastructure, and public spaces that keep our community moving forward.
 Whether you're repairing streets, maintaining stormwater systems, operating heavy equipment, or responding to emergency calls, you'll play an important role in serving the residents of East Ridge.
 Qualifications:
 ✔️ 18 years of age or older
 ✔️ Valid driver's license
 ✔️ High school diploma or equivalent
 ✔️ Can lift up to 50 pounds
 ✔️ Available to respond to after-hours emergency calls
 ✔️ Experience with roadway maintenance, stormwater infrastructure, asphalt, concrete, mowing, and similar work
 ✔️ Ability to learn or knowledge of operating equipment such as backhoe, mini excavator, and skid steer preferred.
 Our Equipment Operators play an important role in maintaining city streets, responding to emergencies, and improving the quality of life for everyone who calls 
 East Ridge home. 🙌🏼
 📌 Full-Time Position | 40 Hours per Week
 Applications may be submitted online through the City's website. 
 🔗 Apply Online today: www.EastRidgeTN.gov/human_resources.html
 The City of East Ridge is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and a Tennessee Drug-Free Workplace.

 📍 Learn more & Apply online at www.eastridgetn.gov/human_resources.html
 The City of East Ridge is an Equal Opportunity Employer and Tennessee Drug-Free Workplace.

items.title: For James Shackelford, Komatsu North America has been home for 38 years. For his daughter, Keri Simerley, it became the place wher...
items.content_text:
For James Shackelford, Komatsu North America has been home for 38 years. For his daughter, Keri Simerley, it became the place where she chose to build her own career.  

Today, they work together at the same facility, continuing a legacy that spans generations.  

Their story captures what 40 years in Chattanooga is really about: investing in people, creating opportunities and building a future that lasts. Here's to the generations who have shaped Komatsu's legacy and those who will carry it forward.  

#Komatsu40 #BuiltByChattanooga

items.title: Hungary should strengthen public finances and revive growth while boosting climate resilience. The new #OECD Economic Su...
items.content_text:
Hungary should strengthen public finances and revive growth while boosting climate resilience.
 The new #OECD Economic Survey of Hungary 2026 outlines the following key priorities:
 ➡️ Improve public spending efficiency and broaden the tax base to ensure fiscal sustainability.
 ➡️ Increase work opportunities for women and young people through expanded childcare and more inclusive education.
 ➡️ Support the competitiveness of small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) by strengthening workforce skills and facilitating business creation and access to financing.
 ➡️ Strengthen resilience to climate risks by improving insurance coverage and providing incentives for private adaptation investment.
 Read more: https://brnw.ch/21x4mJW
 #BetterPoliciesForBetterLives

Some of the best solutions for our community haven't been tried yet. 🚀
 That's why the Catalyst Fund exists: to accelerate promising, innovative strategies for ALICE households – those that are working hard but still struggle to make ends meet.
 The Catalyst Fund is just one piece of our broader community investments process, which invests $3.3 million across 52 local organizations.
 To learn more about how we're investing in Chattanooga's nonprofit ecosystem and to meet the full list of funded partners, visit https://hubs.la/Q04qt6R20.

Engine 6, Ladder 6, Ladder 7, Squad 7, Engine 8, Battalion 1, Battalion 2, and CFD Investigations responded.

items.title: Hixson residents are safe after an overnight fire at their home. At 4:02 AM on 7/23/26, Blue Shift companies responded t...
items.content_text: Hixson residents are safe after an overnight fire at their home. At 4:02 AM on 7/23/26, Blue Shift companies responded to the 6800 block of Captains Way and found smoke coming from a two-story brick residence. Crews found fire in the attic and made an interior attack, resulting in a quick stop. Two occupants had exited the structure and called 911. They were outside on CFD’s arrival. There were no injuries. Engine 11, Squad 19, Ladder 19, Engine 22, Engine 16, and Battalion 3 responded.

items.title: Young people are cast as the face of climate action, so why are they among the most underrepresented voices in London's green tran...
items.content_text:
Young people are cast as the face of climate action, so why are they among the most underrepresented voices in London's green transition? 

We're taking that question to City Hall.  

🌱 Re-thinking green transitions with young Londoners 
🗓 Wednesday 23 September 2026, 10am–2pm 
📍 City Hall, London 

Over the past year, a team of peer researchers from across the capital has been exploring why young Londoners are so often left out of decisions on climate, nature, green jobs and infrastructure, and what it would take to change that.  

This half-day conference shares their findings and turns them into action, convening local authority participation leads and officers, youth climate activists, councillors, funders, civil society and academics. 

In partnership with Partnership for Young London and Islington Council. 

Register your interest now:  https://lnkd.in/eTZfsUFk 

#GreenTransition #YouthClimateAction #London 

Partnership for Young London Islington Council Imogen Hamilton-Jones Matthew Walsham Monika Milewska Phool Ashraf Jakaria Uddin Zoë Wilkins Sophie V.

2026 ADA Celebration Vendor Spotlight: Siskin Children’s Institute
 Siskin Children’s Institute works to improve the quality of life for children with special needs and their families.
 At Siskin Children's Institute families gain access to medical diagnostic and therapy services designed to help children build confidence, strength, mobility, communication, and everyday life skills through individualized, evidence-based care. Our team works alongside families to support developmental milestones and celebrate every step of progress.

Visit siskin.org to learn more about these and other services like our Applied Behavior Analysis Therapy program for children 2-12 years.

items.title: For the past three weeks, Southeast Conservation Corps Youth Crew 957 has been laying the groundwork for a brand-new tra...
items.content_text:
For the past three weeks, Southeast Conservation Corps Youth Crew 957 has been laying the groundwork for a brand-new trail at the up-and-coming Provence Street Park.
 When this project began, the area was heavily overrun with invasive plants. Thanks to our partnership with Wild Violet Permaculture, the site was prepped and cleared, allowing the crew to jump in and do the heavy lifting of constructing the actual path.
 We’re so proud of these crew members for their dedication and hard work over the last several weeks, and deeply grateful for our partnership with SECC for helping make this project possible.
 (Look closely at the bottom of the first photo. Can you spot the SECC crew hard at work? It really puts the scale of this project into perspective! 🔍)

items.title: HCSO Involved in Vehicle Pursuit Yesterday with Successful PIT Maneuver, Suspect Arrested in Georgia Hamilton County, TN...
items.content_text:
HCSO Involved in Vehicle Pursuit Yesterday with Successful PIT Maneuver, Suspect Arrested in Georgia
 Hamilton County, TN - Yesterday, on July 21, 2026, detectives from the HCSO Criminal Warrants Unit located a suspect with active felony warrants out of Walker County, Georgia. As they attempted to take the subject into custody, he entered his vehicle and fled. Deputies pursued the vehicle down Rossville Boulevard to I-24.
 The suspect continued west on I-24, driving erratically, and eventually crossed the TN-GA border. Once the vehicle slowed, an HCSO deputy was able to safely conduct a PIT maneuver on the suspect’s vehicle.
 Due to the pursuit ending in Georgia, the suspect, identified as Kennie Montieth, was transferred into the custody of Georgia authorities. Montieth was charged with the outstanding felony warrants in Georgia, and additional charges were obtained in both Tennessee and Georgia.
 Charges in Dade County, GA
 • Aggravated Assault Police Officer
 • Improper Lane Change/Failure to Maintain Lane
 • No Insurance
 • Willful Obstruction of Law Enforcement Officers by Use of Threats or Violence- Felony
 • Willful Obstruction of Law Enforcement Officers-Misdemeanor
 New Charges in Hamilton County:
 • Evading Arrest
 • Reckless Endangerment
 • Aggravated Assault
 • Driving an Unregistered Vehicle
 • Driving Without a License
 • Speeding
 • Failure to Maintain Lane
 • Reckless Driving 
 Due to the suspect being taken into custody in Georgia we will not be providing the mugshot of Mr. Montieth, please reach out to the Dade County Sheriff’s Office for further information. 
 This information is preliminary and subject to change. No further details are available.

items.title: Did you know that over 1,000 children have died from heatstroke in vehicles since 1998?  More than half of these tragedi...
items.content_text:
Did you know that over 1,000 children have died from heatstroke in vehicles since 1998? 
 More than half of these tragedies occurred when a child was unintentionally forgotten in the back seat. These deaths can be prevented! 
 If you see a child alone in a vehicle, check on them immediately. If they are unresponsive or seem to be in distress, call 911 right away. 
 For more information, visit https://www.nhtsa.gov/campaign/heatstroke

items.content_text:
What do you want to see for the future of Chattanooga's parks and public transportation? Now through mid-August, we are partnering with Google's Jigsaw and Change.org to pilot Civic Dialogue — an AI-powered community survey that continues the conversation in real time as you take it.
 Once the survey closes, Sensemaking AI technology will identify common priorities among residents. A group of residents will then be selected to participate in a virtual discussion, creating policy proposals to be presented to the Kelly Administration. Share your input: https://cha.city/civic-dialogue

items.title: On Friday, July 17, the Advisory Council on Historic Preservation (ACHP) released a proposed rewrite of the federal regu...
items.content_text:
On Friday, July 17, the Advisory Council on Historic Preservation (ACHP) released a proposed rewrite of the federal regulations implementing Section 106 of the National Historic Preservation Act, one of the nation's most important tools for protecting historic places.
 If adopted, these changes would dramatically reduce public participation and shift much of the decision-making authority to federal agencies. Among the proposed changes are:
 • Limiting opportunities for meaningful consultation with State Historic Preservation Offices, Tribal Nations, local governments, and preservation organizations.
 • Narrowing when historic resources are reviewed and what qualifies for protection.
 • Weakening requirements to avoid, minimize, or mitigate impacts to historic places.
 For more than 60 years, Section 106 has helped balance infrastructure, economic development, and community growth with the preservation of our nation's historic and cultural resources. It has never been about stopping projects. It has been about making better decisions through collaboration.
 Learn more at: https://culturalheritagepartners.com/war-room-for-threats-…/
 These proposed changes could affect how historic places across Tennessee, including those here in Chattanooga, are identified, reviewed, and protected. Stay informed, share this post, and follow Preserve Chattanooga as we continue advocating for preservation policies that give our community a voice in shaping its future.
 #ThisIsHistoric #thisishistoric 
 #HistoricPreservation #Section106 #NationalHistoricPreservationAct #PreserveChattanooga #PreservationMatters #ProtectHistoricPlaces

items.title: Responsible business conduct (RBC) is about making sure that the activities of businesses and investors are aligned with...
items.content_text:
Responsible business conduct (RBC) is about making sure that the activities of businesses and investors are aligned with the needs of society, today and in the future.
 The #OECD looked at the world’s 10 000 largest listed public companies to assess their commitment to responsible business.
 7 out of 10 of them had a commitment on at least one responsible business issue, such as reducing pollution and fighting corruption.
 But ambition is not yet translated into action:
 ➡️ More than one third of them have a cross-cutting commitment to RBC spanning all social topics as well as GHG emissions and corruption.
 ➡️ Less than half of them have committed to freedom of association, which guarantees the right of individuals to form, join and participate in associations, groups, movements, and civil society organisations.
 ➡️ Only 1 in 4 have commitments to biodiversity
 How can governments increase uptake and ensure that commitments are put into practice?
 Learn more in the OECD Responsible Business Outlook ➡️ https://brnw.ch/21x4loa
 #OECDResponsibleBusinessConduct

items.title: The City of Collegedale has created an advisory Airport Board, and the City is soliciting Letters of Interest from candi...
items.content_text:
The City of Collegedale has created an advisory Airport Board, and the City is soliciting Letters of Interest from candidates who would like to serve on that board. 
 The Airport Board is tasked by the Collegedale City Commission with recommending such rules and regulations concerning the operation and use of the Collegedale Municipal Airport to the Collegedale City commission for consideration and formal adoption as the City Commission may deem necessary and appropriate. Such rules shall be recommended as may be consistent with the public interest and the public health, safety and general welfare of the airport and the Collegedale community.  The Collegedale Tennessee Airport Board will be advisory in capacity to the City Commission.
 City residency is not required for all appointments.  The Board consists of five (5) individuals.  The Board makeup must consist of three (3) citizens whose primary residence is within the City Limits of the City of Collegedale, and two (2) citizens may be appointed that live outside the city limits of the City of Collegedale. Residents appointed to the Airport Board shall serve for a three-year term.  Non-Residents shall serve for an initial one-year term, and future reappointments will be for two-year terms.  
 To be considered for appointment to the Airport Board, please submit a Letter of Interest that provides the following: 
 1. Your primary residential address and indicate whether it is located inside or outside the city limits of the City of Collegedale. 
 2. A brief statement about why you are interested in serving on Airport Board. 
 3. Any information about yourself, your qualifications and experiences that you believe would be beneficial for appointment to the Airport Advisory Board. A resume’ would be acceptable for this information. 
 Interested parties may submit their Letter of Interest in person or as an attachment to an email no later than 12 Noon, on Friday August 21st.  
 To submit your Letter of Interest in person please submit it at the Collegedale City Hall no later than 12 Noon, Friday August 21st: 
 Collegedale City Hall 4910 Swinyar Drive Collegedale, TN 37315-1880 
 To submit your Letter of Interest as an attachment to an email, please send it to kwheeler@collegedaletn.gov no later than 12 Noon Friday August 21st.
items.content_html:
The City of Collegedale has created an advisory Airport Board, and the City is soliciting Letters of Interest from candidates who would like to serve on that board.
The Airport Board is tasked by the Collegedale City Commission with recommending such rules and regulations concerning the operation and use of the Collegedale Municipal Airport to the Collegedale City commission for consideration and formal adoption as the City Commission may deem necessary and appropriate. Such rules shall be recommended as may be consistent with the public interest and the public health, safety and general welfare of the airport and the Collegedale community. The Collegedale Tennessee Airport Board will be advisory in capacity to the City Commission.
City residency is not required for all appointments. The Board consists of five (5) individuals. The Board makeup must consist of three (3) citizens whose primary residence is within the City Limits of the City of Collegedale, and two (2) citizens may be appointed that live outside the city limits of the City of Collegedale. Residents appointed to the Airport Board shall serve for a three-year term. Non-Residents shall serve for an initial one-year term, and future reappointments will be for two-year terms.

items.title: They say we’re the "Department of Fun," but behind every smile we create is a huge team of incredible people working har...
items.content_text:
They say we’re the "Department of Fun," but behind every smile we create is a huge team of incredible people working hard to make our city shine.
 Today is National Employee Appreciation Day, and "thank you" feels like an understatement. Maintaining our lush parks, restoring natural habitats, leading outdoor adventures, ensuring recreation is accessible for everyone, facilitating sports, leading kids' camps, throwing movies in the parks, and SO much more behind the scenes, these are the faces that make our city feel like home.
 We celebrated this past Wednesday with an afternoon of well-deserved camaraderie. It’s a small token of gratitude for a team that gives so much.
 Help us celebrate them! If you see our crew out and about, give them a wave or a quick "thank you." This work is big, it’s rewarding, and we couldn’t do it without them.

items.content_text:
Early-bird registration is now open for Chattanooga Connect 2026, the UTC at Chattanooga Research Institute’s (UTCRI) third annual conference that brings together leaders from government, industry, academia and entrepreneurship to explore the future of transportation, digital infrastructure, artificial intelligence, quantum technologies and connected mobility.
 Hosted Sept. 14-16 in Chattanooga, Chattanooga Connect will convene national experts, researchers, policymakers and industry leaders to share how emerging technologies are moving beyond research and pilot projects into real-world deployment. The three-day event will feature keynote presentations, panel discussions, networking opportunities, technology demonstrations and collaborative discussions focused on advancing practical, scalable solutions to some of today’s most pressing infrastructure and mobility challenges.
 🔗  Read more here: https://blog.utc.edu/…/registration-opens-for-third-annual…/

items.title: 📈 Africa is projected to account for 85% of global labour force growth by 2050. Its economy grew by nearly 4% a year be...
items.content_text:
📈 Africa is projected to account for 85% of global labour force growth by 2050. Its economy grew by nearly 4% a year between 2000 and 2020, above the global average. 
 The Africa’s Development Dynamics 2025 report finds that the continent could more than double its GDP by 2040 if annual infrastructure investment rises to USD 155 billion. 
 Yet Africa holds just 1.75% of the world’s inward foreign direct investment stock. 
 Limited access to reliable and comparable information can make it harder to assess investment opportunities and risks across countries and sectors. 
 The African Virtual Investment Platform (AVIP), jointly developed by the African Union Commission and the OECD and endorsed by the G7 in 2024, brings together data and indicators covering: 
 ✅ Foreign and domestic investment 
 ✅ Public and private investment 
 ✅ Policies that shape investment across the continent 
 More than 15 African countries have joined the pilot phase, contributing data and helping shape how investment is measured and compared. As AVIP expands towards continental coverage, additional country participation will broaden the data available through the platform. 
 🔗 𝗔𝗩𝗜𝗣 𝗶𝘀 𝗻𝗼𝘄 𝗹𝗶𝘃𝗲. 𝗘𝘅𝗽𝗹𝗼𝗿𝗲 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗽𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺. 𝗟𝗶𝗻𝗸 𝗶𝗻 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀. ⤵️ 
 #AVIP2026 #OECDdevelopment

items.title: The past couple of days at the TNSTRONG Youth Summit have been filled with learning, connection, reflection, and fun! Ou...
items.content_text:
The past couple of days at the TNSTRONG Youth Summit have been filled with learning, connection, reflection, and fun! Our youth participated in powerful sessions focused on tobacco and vaping prevention, mental wellness, youth voice, advocacy, healthy decision-making, and creating positive change in their communities.
 They also had opportunities to share their experiences, participate in interactive activities, strengthen their leadership skills, and connect with other young people from across Tennessee. It always feels good to link up with our fellow friends in prevention who share the same passion for empowering youth and building healthier communities.
 We are heading home inspired, better equipped, and excited to put everything we learned into action. Our young leaders are returning with fresh ideas, stronger voices, and an even greater commitment to creating positive change in Robertson County. This was more than just a summit; it was another step toward preparing them to lead meaningful prevention efforts in their schools and throughout our community.
 Tobacco-free teens build stronger communities, and empowered youth voices are leading the way toward a healthier future. 💚
 #TNSTRONG #TNSTRONGYouthSummit #YouthPrevention #PoweredByPrevention #YouthVoice #ChoZen #RobertsonCounty

items.title: HCSO Arrests Suspect for Creation of CSAM (Child Sex Abuse Material) Hamilton County, TN - On July 21, 2026, detectives...
items.content_text:
HCSO Arrests Suspect for Creation of CSAM (Child Sex Abuse Material)
 Hamilton County, TN - On July 21, 2026, detectives with the Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office Criminal Investigations Unit arrested Zackary Nelson regarding the manufacture of Child Sexual Abuse Material (CSAM). 
 A search warrant was executed at Nelson’s residence, and numerous electronic devices were seized. He was taken into custody at Daisy Elementary School, where he worked as a janitor.
 Nelson was transported to the Hamilton County Jail & Detention Center and is being charged with the following:
 Sexual Exploitation of a Minor
 The HCSO Criminal Investigations Unit has a message for anyone who chooses to victimize our county's youth:  "If you choose to exploit children from a computer, remember that we still make house calls. Don’t be surprised when you look up from the screen and see us."
 This information is preliminary and subject to change. No further details are available.

items.title: The City of Collegedale is excited to welcome McKenna Wallace as our new Communication & Engagement Manager. She will be...
items.content_text:
The City of Collegedale is excited to welcome McKenna Wallace as our new Communication & Engagement Manager. She will begin her role in early August.
 McKenna is a graduate of East Tennessee State University (ETSU), where she earned her Bachelor of Business Administration with a concentration in Marketing Management. She joins us with experience in marketing, event planning, and community engagement, most recently serving as the Events & Marketing Manager for Habitat for Humanity. In her new role, McKenna will help strengthen the City’s communication efforts and build connections with residents and community partners. We are excited about the experience, creativity, and enthusiasm she will bring to our team.
 "One of the things that impressed us most was McKenna's enthusiasm and her demonstrated experience overseeing communication strategies for a nonprofit organization," said City Manager Jon Holmes. "This experience will be beneficial as she transitions to the public sector and leads our efforts to promote communication and transparency for Collegedale citizens and the surrounding community. We are excited to have her on board."
 Outside of work, McKenna enjoys spending time with family and working on crafting projects.
 Please join us in welcoming McKenna Wallace to the City of Collegedale team!

items.title: What exactly is the Southeast Tennessee Development District (SETD)? It's a question we hear quite often. When people hear the ter...
items.content_text:
What exactly is the Southeast Tennessee Development District (SETD)?

It's a question we hear quite often.

When people hear the term "Development District," they're often surprised to learn just how many programs, services, and partnerships are coordinated through SETD.

As a regional organization serving 10 counties across Southeast Tennessee, we work alongside the state and local governments, community partners, businesses, and residents to help strengthen communities and improve quality of life throughout our region.

From supporting older adults and caregivers to improving workforce development, transportation and regional planning, tourism, community and economic development, grant assistance, and more, our work reaches communities in ways that often happen behind the scenes.

Check out this quick overview to view the many ways our organization serves Southeast Tennessee.

👉 Swipe through to learn more about the programs and services that make up SETD.

Have questions about one of our programs? We'd love to hear from you.

 AI is changing how people learn, work and increasingly, how they manage their money.
 As it becomes part of everyday financial decision making, it can help consumers access financial information and receive more personalised advice and support.

items.title: Exploring Downtown: Chattanooga City Hall Completed in 1908, Chattanooga City Hall has served as the city's municipal ce...
items.content_text:
Exploring Downtown: Chattanooga City Hall
 Completed in 1908, Chattanooga City Hall has served as the city's municipal center for more than a century. Designed by architect R. H. Hunt in the Classical Revival style, the building reflects an architectural movement that shaped many of Downtown Chattanooga's civic buildings.
 Its symmetrical design and prominent columns draw inspiration from the public buildings of ancient Greece and Rome, a style often associated with civic duty, democracy, and permanence. 
 Today, City Hall remains one of Downtown Chattanooga's most recognizable civic landmarks. It was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1980.

items.content_text:
Some of life's biggest milestones begin in unexpected places.  

For Randy and Brandee Coppinger, Komatsu North America is where they met, built careers together and raised their family.  More than 30 years later, they're still part of the team that has helped make Komatsu Chattanooga what it is today.  

Their story is a reminder that the strongest companies are built by strong relationships, both on and off the job. 

#Komatsu40 #BuiltByChattanooga

items.content_text:
Today, the City of Chattanooga and Hamilton County launched Bring Back the BLVD — an initiative that will spark the revitalization of one of Chattanooga’s main gateways! 
 We are advocating to make this area an Opportunity Zone under the State of Tennessee’s Opportunity Zones 2.0 program, which if approved, creates federal tax incentives to encourage private investment in existing properties, transforming vacant and underutilized sites. The Opportunity Zone recommendation now moves to the State for consideration.

items.title: HCSO SWAT Unit Successfully Apprehends Aggravated Assault Suspect Hamilton County, TN – On the evening of July 20, 2026,...
items.content_text:
HCSO SWAT Unit Successfully Apprehends Aggravated Assault Suspect
 Hamilton County, TN – On the evening of July 20, 2026, the Soddy-Daisy Police Department requested the help of HCSO deputies after an aggravated assault suspect refused to exit a residence at the 8400 block of Springfield Road.
 The Soddy-Daisy Police Department believed the suspect, Jerry Eustice Jr., to be armed, and Eustice has a history of violent behavior. The HCSO SWAT Unit responded to the scene, securing the area to protect the neighbors and successfully taking Eustice into custody. He was turned over to the Soddy-Daisy Police Department for further investigation.
 This reflects the mission of the HCSO SWAT Unit: to preserve life during high-risk situations through specialized training, equipment, and tactics.
 All questions regarding the aggravated assault investigation or criminal charges should be directed to the Soddy-Daisy Police Department.
 This information is preliminary and subject to change. No further details are available.

items.title: State enterprises play a major role in industrial subsidies. Companies with more than 25% government ownership tend to r...
items.content_text:
State enterprises play a major role in industrial subsidies.
 Companies with more than 25% government ownership tend to receive relatively more:
  ➜ Government grants
  ➜ Below-market borrowings 
 This is especially common in heavy industries, where firms depend more heavily on debt financing and support from state banks.
 Learn more about the role of state enterprises in industrial subsidies: https://brnw.ch/21x4isF

items.title: What a wonderful evening at the 91st Annual Chattanooga Ball! Keith and I were honored to be invited to sit at the King’...
items.content_text:
What a wonderful evening at the 91st Annual Chattanooga Ball!
 Keith and I were honored to be invited to sit at the King’s table as we celebrated King Alan Shuptrine. It was such a joy to support Alan and his beautiful wife, Bonny, who have given so much of themselves to our community over the years. Their generosity and servant hearts are a blessing to so many, and this recognition was truly well deserved.
 Congratulations to King Alan Shuptrine, Queen Reese Hullander, the Belles, and everyone who helped make the evening so special. It was wonderful to spend the evening with friends, celebrate a great Chattanooga tradition, and see so many people come together to give back to our community.
 Alan and Bonny, thank you for your gracious hospitality. Keith and I had a wonderful time and were grateful to celebrate this special honor with you both. ❤️

items.title: Thank you for the birthday wishes! At first I struggled with turning an age closer to 50, but my son Blaize, Tenice, and...
items.content_text:
Thank you for the birthday wishes! At first I struggled with turning an age closer to 50, but my son Blaize, Tenice, and Chasyn called and we actually figured it wasn't so bad aging. It only means I am getting closer to that elder status and the benefits that come with it 😆 anywho... Spent my birthday heading to TNSTRONG YOUTH SUMMIT with a few of our amazing Unicoi SADD students, Greeneville County and Johnson County crews. If I can't be with my family, might as well spend it with my extended family 😁 
 But before my send off, my girls Ten, Evie, and TC cooked me breakfast, and my daughter made me a beautiful picture 🥹 along with a basket full of goodies, and got my boys to eat some breakfast with me. The Coalition crews and SADD girls got me some gifts as well. Feeling the Love!

items.title: 🎉Special Exhibit🎉 "What If: Designs from the Future of Crafting & Growing" August 8, 2026
items.content_text:
🎉Special Exhibit🎉
 "What If: Designs from the Future of Crafting & Growing"
 August 8, 2026 - September 26, 2026
 Opening Reception and Artist Talk August 8 at 1:30pm
 FREE During Museum Hours
 An exhibit by Humanities Tennessee’s Shared Futures Lab, features artifacts and stories from the future imagined by seven Tennessee artists and farmers about their work. 
 The speculative designs invite the public to imagine the broad impact of possible change–from sports to building, burial to celebrity–and how it may shape our future communities.
 Curated by
 Liza Blair
 Curator and Museum Specialist
 Denise Heinly Art Center and Outdoor Museum of Art
 Chattanooga State Community College
 “Imagining Future Stories” Opening Reception and Artist Talk 
 August 8, 2026 | 1:30pm - 3pm
 FREE
 Join What If…? curator Liza Blair in conversation with crafters & growers for a deeper dive into the future stories they imagined and the artifacts and ideas that inhabit them.
 Panelists include:
 Julia Donagrandi, Loof'n Round Gardens, (Loofahs Grown to Enhance Our Home | Loof'n Round Gardens)
 Annie LaVoie, Mouthy Oddities, Home | Life is beautiful, even after death.™
 Sheila-Renese Williams, Moore Family Century Farm. Moore Family Century Farm | schedule farm tour | Holladay, TN, USA
 Sponsored by Humanities Tennessee and TN Arts Commission.

items.content_text:
Two UTC College of Nursing faculty members recently shared their work on AI-assisted simulation with an international audience, leading a webinar on how the technology can help prepare graduate nurse practitioner students for difficult conversations in palliative care.
 Dr. Chris Doneski, director of UTC’s acute care nurse practitioner program and a simulation faculty member, and Dr. Rosebelle Peters, nursing simulation program director and senior lecturer, presented “Prepare Graduate Nurse Practitioners for Difficult Conversations with AI Palliative Simulations” through HealthySimulation.com.
 🔗  Learn more here: https://blog.utc.edu/…/difficult-discussions-how-utc-nursi…/

items.title: After decades of searching and speculation, we finally found him!  Skeptics left aghast after Bigfoot was discovered in...
items.content_text:
After decades of searching and speculation, we finally found him! 
 Skeptics left aghast after Bigfoot was discovered in a local park! We are lucky he stopped and posed for a quick photo op before scurrying off like Jimothy the raccoon, his inspiration and idol.
 Our staff have affectionately named this hunk of metal Freddie the Yeti, and he's looking for his owner. If you know who left him at the park, please reach out to us so we can safely get him back home. This is why you should always microchip your pets!
 While we are grateful for his patronage to our parks, we can't allow him to loiter. We have a search and rescue operation scheduled for this evening, and he will seek temporary solace in our lost and found at Outdoor Chattanooga. We look forward to a tearful reunification. 
 Help us share this post so it reaches his owner! If you're the owner, please DM us with Freddy's posted location and we will get him back to you, or come to Outdoor Chattanooga starting tomorrow, 7/21, with proof of ownership (vet records, baby photos, etc...) to claim.

items.title: A new chapter for one of Chattanooga’s historic corridors? Hamilton County Mayor Weston Wamp, Chattanooga Mayor Tim Kell...
items.content_text:
A new chapter for one of Chattanooga’s historic corridors?
 Hamilton County Mayor Weston Wamp, Chattanooga Mayor Tim Kelly, and community leaders have launched Bring Back the BLVD, an initiative focused on attracting investment, supporting local businesses, and restoring Rossville Boulevard as a thriving commercial corridor.
 For generations, Rossville Boulevard has been more than just a road — it has been a connection between communities, commerce, and history. When dedicated in April 1912, it was considered the city's finest stretch of roadway. A parade marked the occasion. In the 1920's, a portion of the Boulevard was designated a "Road of Remembrance" to honor soldiers. Two stone columns at 32nd Street still mark the entrance. The bronze plaque reads "FOUR AMERICAN ARMIES HAVE MARCHED OVER THIS ROAD. TWO OPPOSING ARMIES IN THE CIVIL WAR 1861-1865. ONE IN THE SPANISH AMERICAN WAR 1898. AND THE LAST IN THE WORLD WAR 1917-1918." Over the decades, the Boulevard became home to neighborhood businesses, industries, auto dealerships, and gathering places that reflected the diversity and entrepreneurial spirit of our city.
 Like many historic commercial corridors across America, Rossville Boulevard has faced challenges as development patterns changed. The proposed Opportunity Zone designation could help bring new attention and resources to this important gateway while honoring the people, businesses, and stories that have shaped the Boulevard for more than a century.
 At Preserve Chattanooga, we believe historic places are not just about the past, they are about creating stronger communities for the future. We look forward to seeing how preservation, investment, and community vision can work together to bring back the BLVD.
 Read more about this exciting initiative: https://www.chattanoogan.com/…/City-County-Partnering-To-Re…
 #PreserveChattanooga #HistoricChattanooga #RossvilleBoulevard #BringBackTheBLVD

🌧️🚜 Stormwater Team Hard at Work After the Weekend Storms 🚜🌧️ After the heavy rainfall that Red Bank experienced ove...
items.content_text:
🌧️🚜 Stormwater Team Hard at Work After the Weekend Storms 🚜🌧️
 After the heavy rainfall that Red Bank experienced over the weekend, the City's Public Works Stormwater team has been working to assess drainage infrastructure and remove accumulated debris along Stringers Branch. This helps to restore the stream to its full flow capacity and reduce the risk of future blockages occurring, which can cause localized flooding. 
 If you notice a clogged drain, blocked ditch, or excessive debris in our public drainage infrastructure, please let us know! If you see something that our team needs to respond to, you can submit a work order at https://redbanktn.portal.iworq.net/portalhome/redbanktn or call 423-805-7059. Reporting these issues helps us to respond quickly and ensures that we're able to protect our community from flooding.
 Thank you to our Stormwater team for their dedication and hard work in keeping Red Bank's waterways and drainage infrastructure functioning properly after severe weather. 
 #RedBankTN #PublicWorks #StormwaterManagement #FloodPrevention

items.content_text:
The future of manufacturing isn't coming—it's already here. The question is: Are you ready for it?
 At ChattState, our Industrial Automation & Robotics Technician program prepares you to build, maintain, and troubleshoot the advanced systems that keep modern industry moving. From robotics and PLCs to automation controls, hydraulics, pneumatics, and Industry 4.0 technologies, you'll gain hands-on skills employers are looking for.
 Whether you're starting a new career or leveling up your technical skills, ChattState's TCAT can help you become the technician industries depend on.
 Financial aid including free tuition available to those who qualify. 
 Build your career now at chattanoogastate.edu/tcat  
 Video description: Two ChattState students work in the TCAT industrial lab with electrical boards and robotics.

items.title: Today was a big day for the Rossville Boulevard district. Our company is still considered part of this area from the int...
items.content_text:
Today was a big day for the Rossville Boulevard district. Our company is still considered part of this area from the interstate to the state line. But what makes this almost as exciting is that our original location on McFarland, where we were before I was born, touches this area as well. When this group first met, it was a very small group of us at The Barn Nursery. Today there was a press release with both mayors and every local news outlet.  
 Huge thanks to our commissioner and our wonderful council woman and both mayors for coming together on this. We are excited to see public and private investment, especially as we plan our company’s investments year by year that we continue to make in TBN and our surrounding community! Tim Kelly Weston Wamp Raquetta Dotley Jim Webster Cindy Watkins Webster Jody Shea Coralee Buckels Bryan Evans Lumber Crabtree Farms of Chattanooga
